plan_metadata.name: Feature Simple Bot Remote Template Verification

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the dropdown under "Agents for Microsoft 365 Copilot" to initiate creating or managing bots, apps, or web pages using the Microsoft Teams SDK.
Click the "Other Teams Capabilities" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK" section.
Click the "Simple Bot" option within the dropdown menu labeled "Teams Capability" in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Default folder" field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects".
Click on the "m365agents.yml" file in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to select and open it.
@assertion "version: v1.8" doesn't exist
Click the "Close" (X) button on the "m365agents.yml" file tab in the Visual Studio Code editor interface to close the file.
Press the keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+~ in Visual Studio Code to open a new terminal while on the "Build a Declarative Agent" welcome page in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension.
Type the text 'export TEMPLATE_VERSION=5.0.3' into the integrated Terminal panel within Visual Studio Code, in a workspace running bash.
Press the Enter key in the integrated Terminal of Visual Studio Code after entering the command `export TEMPLATE_VERSION=5.0.3` in a bash session.
Type text 'code .' into the integrated Terminal in Visual Studio Code within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace.
Press the Enter key in the integrated Terminal of Visual Studio Code to execute the command `code .`.
Click the "Map" extension icon in the left sidebar of Visual Studio Code to open the Map extension panel.
Press the Esc key to dismiss the "An OS keyring couldn't be identified" encryption warning popup at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.

Click the "Create a New Agent/App" blue button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit sidebar within the Visual Studio Code interface. The interaction point is visually indicated by a red crosshair centered on the button, ensuring precise selection of the action to start a new agent or app creation workflow.
Click the "Teams Agents and Apps – Apps for Microsoft 365" option in the "New Project" dialog within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension in Visual Studio Code.

Click the "Other Teams Capabilities" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Microsoft Teams SDK" selection dialog in Visual Studio Code (Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension).
Click the "Tab" option in the Teams Capability dropdown within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown within Visual Studio Code when initializing a Microsoft 365 Agents project.
Click the "Default folder" option (/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects) in the "Workspace Folder" selection dialog within Visual Studio Code, to select the project root folder location.
Type  '${{var:app_name:vscuse_app_#####}}'  into the "Input an application name" text field in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension popup within Visual Studio Code.
Type text 'test' into the 'Application Name' input field within the Visual Studio Code application’s modal dialog.
Press the Enter key to confirm the application name input in the VS Code application name dialog.
Press the Escape (Esc) key to dismiss the notification popup confirming successful app creation by the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit in Visual Studio Code.
Click the "m365agents.yml" file in the Explorer panel of Visual Studio Code to open it.
@assertion "version: v1.8" exists
